req.title: Standing order (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall set up a standing order with start date in the future; past start dates shall be rejected. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that standing orders must have a future start_date, with past/today dates rejected (HTTP 422). Four test cases are designed: (1) EP-positive: a clearly valid future date (today+30d) confirms the happy path; (2) NEG-negative: a clearly past date (today-30d) confirms rejection; (3) BVA-boundary: exact today (boundary ON point) must be rejected; (4) BVA-boundary: tomorrow (boundary OFF point, first valid value) must be accepted. Together these give EP, NEG, and BVA coverage as required by ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4 boundary and equivalence partitioning techniques. All tests are self-contained via the `auth_headers` and `api_base_url` fixtures and use deterministic date arithmetic relative to the runtime date.

tests.name: test_standing_order_valid_future_date
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid bearer token available via auth_headers fixture)
Banking API is reachable at api_base_url
actions: POST /standing-orders with valid payload and future start_date
expected: Response body contains an order id
Response body start_date matches the submitted date
trace: R-FIN-045: order scheduled when start_date is in the future
EP: representative value from valid (future) partition
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_valid_future_date(auth_headers, api_base_url):
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": 100,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date,
        "beneficiary_account": "<valid_account>"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain an order id"
    assert body.get("start_date") == start_date

tests.name: test_standing_order_past_date_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid bearer token available via auth_headers fixture)
Banking API is reachable at api_base_url
actions: Compute start_date as today - 30 days (clearly in the past)
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing start_date
trace: R-FIN-045: past start dates shall be rejected with 422
NEG: representative value from invalid (past) partition
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_past_date_rejected(auth_headers, api_base_url):
    start_date = (date.today() - timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": 100,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date,
        "beneficiary_account": "<valid_account>"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "start_date" in error_text, "Error body must reference start_date field"

tests.name: test_standing_order_today_boundary_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid bearer token available via auth_headers fixture)
Banking API is reachable at api_base_url
actions: Compute start_date as exactly today (boundary ON point: start_date == today)
POST /standing-orders with start_date equal to today
expected: Response confirms today is not an accepted start_date
trace: R-FIN-045: start_date <= today shall be rejected; today is the exact boundary ON point
BVA: ON-point of the invalid region (start_date <= today)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date
import requests

def test_standing_order_today_boundary_rejected(auth_headers, api_base_url):
    start_date = date.today().isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": 100,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date,
        "beneficiary_account": "<valid_account>"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, (
        f"today ({start_date}) must be rejected; got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "start_date" in str(body).lower(), "Error body must reference start_date"

tests.name: test_standing_order_tomorrow_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid bearer token available via auth_headers fixture)
Banking API is reachable at api_base_url
actions: Compute start_date as today + 1 day (boundary OFF point: first valid value)
POST /standing-orders with start_date equal to tomorrow
expected: Response body contains an order id
Response body start_date matches tomorrow's date
trace: R-FIN-045: start_date strictly in the future must be accepted; tomorrow is the minimum valid value
BVA: OFF-point of the valid region (start_date == today+1)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, timedelta
import requests

def test_standing_order_tomorrow_boundary_accepted(auth_headers, api_base_url):
    start_date = (date.today() + timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": 100,
        "currency": "GBP",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": start_date,
        "beneficiary_account": "<valid_account>"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/standing-orders",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"tomorrow ({start_date}) must be accepted; got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain an order id"
    assert body.get("start_date") == start_date
